column5: DJ Younger Twin got the St. Patty's started last month, and I'm continuing with several suggestions this month. Suffice it to say you haven't lived until you've been countless Guinesses deep at an Irish pub and get to scream sing this with all of your new closest friends.
column6: Big fan of the claps - DJ Midge
column7: @DJ Midge just remember, the claps go 4 - 2 - 1.  If you mess it up, the entire bar heckles at you and you have to take a drink! - DJ Younger Twin
column8: If it's a bar in Ireland they'll already be beating Chuck's ass for calling it St. Patty's Day - Schiff

column5: Hard to pick just one Gaelic Storm song, but this is the first one of theirs I remember knowing I knew. (They're the party in third class band in Titanic, but I didn't know that for awhile). Anyhoo, the moon is a good friend, thanks for the light, big guy.
column6: Love me some Gaelic Storm. A lot of great songs by them!  They're coming to the Birchmere at the end of March! - DJ Younger Twin

column5: Rounding out the Irish jams with a trad song. Lúnasa is THE band for Irish trad as far as I'm concerned and this song always makes me happy and ready to ramble through the lush green hills of old Ireland.

This one popped up on an IG reel like two weeks ago and it's been pretty much playing on repeat since.  Waited to put it on the March playlist because of St Pats. BRB.... going to go hang out in my Irish sweater staring off towards the sea.
column6: I'm going to need a painting of you in your sweater, staring at the sea - DJ Chunk

column5: El Paso made me think of this song, which is at the end of The Hateful Eight (great movie by the way).  I don't really have anything else to say than it continues my story of the songs - other than there's the gunfight at the end of El Paso - so maybe there won't be many coming home?
column6: These songs: holy shit depressing - DJ Chunk

column5: The song above this made me think of this song - so here it is.  Who can't like Tom Jones?  Could this have been the song in the head of the subject of El Paso?
Another song I thought of when I put the one above this on the list.  I guess all my songs this month are somewhat melancholic?

Changing it up with a more upbeat song (by tying to my first song about an Arizona Ranger) - this is a song from Tik Tok or Instagram - but I think it's catchy.  So there you have it - we'll be back to more upbeat songs next month.
